pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something and then i remember. the silence is actually physically painful. i don't even know why i bother waking up and doing all this stuff every day when the person i actually lived for isn't even here anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost haunting my own house.

pool.baseline: i was looking at some photos of deep space tonight and i just feel... empty. like, everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny flicker in a universe that is actually just cold and dead. does it ever feel ridiculous to you? like we're all just performing these tiny, meaningless dramas while the universe doesn't even care?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this. i just feel like i've completely hit a wall. after 5 years of this phd, the funding just... it's gone. the lab is basically empty now. i look at my desk and i just feel nothing. no motivation, no future, just this massive, heavy emptiness. it feels like i wasted my entire life for a version of myself that doesn't even exist anymore. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for something that clearly isn't meant to be.

pool.baseline: The candles are all guttering out, and the house is so terribly quiet now that he is gone. Everything feels... hollow. Like a ghost haunting my own life. I look at the moon and I don't see light anymore, just a cold, empty void that calls to me. It almost seems so much more peaceful to just... stop. To let the darkness take everything so I don't have to feel this ache anymore. It feels like the only way to truly find rest.

Would anyone even notice the silence?
